channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Amazon is ending support for older Kindles and Kindle Fires
item.description: Amazon has announced that starting on May 20th, 2026, Kindle e-readers and Kindle Fire devices released in 2012 and earlier will "no longer be able to purchase, borrow, or download new content via the Kindle Store," Amazon spokesperson Jackie Burke wrote in an email to The Verge. Users will still be able to read books […]

item.title: I can’t help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee
item.description: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

item.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups
item.description: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

item.title: 'Stolen session cookies render MFA irrelevant': How $900-per-month turnkey malware is putting enterprise-grade account hijacking in the hands of rookie hackers
item.description: Storm infostealer hijacks session cookies, bypassing multi-factor authentication, harvesting credentials, and enabling persistent account access across enterprise and cryptocurrency systems globally.

item.title: Fox is the latest to add prediction markets as a new ‘data layer’ for news coverage
item.description: FOX, CNBC, and CNN now have Kalshi forecasts integrated into their platforms. Fox Corporation has announced plans to partner with Kalshi to integrate the prediction market’s data across the media giant’s various cable networks.

item.title: X has a slightly more functional photo editor now
item.description: X is rolling out an update to its in-app photo editor that gives users the ability to edit photos with xAI's Grok, blur faces and overlay text on images. The new editing features, in particular the addition of text-based edits via an AI assistant, bring it much closer in capabilities to dedicated photo apps like Google Photos. As part of the update, users are able to prompt Grok to make edits to a photo just by typing out what they want to see. The example video shared by Nikita Bier, X's Head of Product, showed an image being edited so that it appeared to be hanging in a museum, but simpler t...

item.title: On the day that Apple turned 50, the iconic US company that inspired Steve Jobs with the GUI hit rock bottom and may be on the path to obscurity
item.description: The contrast between Apple’s 50th anniversary and Xerox leadership change shows how early innovation alone rarely secures lasting relevance.

item.title: I love my Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses but this summer I’m gonna swap them for standard shades — and the BanRay movement proves I’m not alone
item.description: "Meta cannot be trusted with this" — an anti-smartglasses movement is brewing.

item.title: Noah Hawley Is Remaking ‘Terrified,’ Which Shouldn’t Happen
item.description: We love the 'Alien: Earth' creator, but Argentinian filmmaker Demián Rugna's original is a one-of-a-kind.

item.title: The iPhone Gets a D– for Repairability
item.description: It’s a better rating than the company has gotten from repairability experts before, at least. Samsung is second worst with a D.

item.title: Unprecedented Observation Reveals 2 Supermassive Black Holes Locked in a Tight Death Spiral
item.description: New research describes an unexpected double jet in a distant galaxy, revealing a pair of supermassive black holes on the verge of colliding.

item.title: I don't care about the iPhone Fold — the biggest moment in iPhone history just happened in space
item.description: The Artemis II crew used an iPhone, and suddenly, nothing is more important about Apple's iconic product, not even the possibility of a foldable device.

item.title: Floating data centers cruise a bit closer after a major show of support from Japanese giant Hitachi
item.description: Japan is advancing floating data center plans using converted ships, with Hitachi support, to address land shortages and growing AI infrastructure demand.

item.title: Anthropic Launches ‘Project Glasswing’ to Stealthily Spot Cybersecurity Issues for Rivals
item.description: The company previously warned that its newest model "presents unprecedented cybersecurity risks."

item.title: Tiny Robots Reveal Hidden Ocean Chemistry in Low-Oxygen Waters
item.description: A trove of robotic floats is bringing our knowledge of the deep sea to new depths.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Are Sabotaging US Energy and Water Infrastructure
item.description: As Trump threatens Iranian infrastructure, the US government warns that Iran has carried out its own digital attacks against US critical infrastructure.

item.title: Spotify’s Prompted Playlists can help you find new podcasts to listen to
item.description: On Tuesday, Spotify expanded its Prompted Playlists feature to include podcasts, an update that could make it easier for Premium users to find new shows to listen to. Prompted Playlists were originally launched as a beta feature in December, but previously only worked for music. You can use the feature to effectively generate customized Discover […]...

item.title: Aunt Lydia Survived ‘The Handmaid’s Tale’ to Bring New Complexity to ‘The Testaments’
item.description: She's no longer framed as a villain—but what's her role in the sequel series? Emmy-winning actor Ann Dowd explains.

item.title: Getting laid off changes your perception of work forever. Here’s how
item.description: Being let go breaks your spirit—and your brain. When Kitty got her fourth layoff call, she took it via Bluetooth in her car. She knew the script by then: the sudden 15-minute meeting invite, the HR rep that pops into the call, the platitudes that precede the devastation of being unemployed—again.

item.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn
item.description: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

item.title: Scientists Think They’ve Finally Discovered Why Your Cat Never Finishes Its Meal
item.description: Turns out there might be a very understandable reason why your cat leaves its bowl half-empty.
